name: Russia

visa_types.name: Unified Electronic Visa (Tourist/Private/Business)
visa_types.purpose: Permits entry for tourism, private visits, business meetings, and participation in scientific, cultural, socio-political, economic, or sporting events. Does not permit employment, study, or permanent residency.
visa_types.notes: Available to citizens of 76 countries approved by the Russian Government. Valid 120 days from date of issue; stay must not exceed 30 days from entry. Children under 6 are exempt from the consular fee. Must enter via designated border crossing points. Application window: no earlier than 86 days and no later than 4 days before intended entry. Medical insurance valid in Russia is required for the entire stay. Extensions permitted only for emergency medical treatment, force majeure, or natural disasters.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (Standard Consular)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Russia for business meetings, negotiations, conferences, exhibitions, and commercial activities. Requires an official invitation from a Russian legal entity or registered through the Chamber of Commerce.
visa_types.notes: Applied for at a Russian embassy or consulate abroad. Invitation (vyzov) required from a Russian host organization registered with the Ministry of Internal Affairs or through the Chamber of Commerce and Industry. Single-entry and multiple-entry variants available; validity and entries depend on bilateral agreement and host organization's application. Fee varies by nationality and processing speed. Business visa does not permit employment - a work permit and work visa are required for that.

visa_types.name: Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: Authorises foreign nationals to undertake paid employment in Russia under a labour contract. Must be sponsored by a Russian employer who has obtained a work permit (razresheniye na rabotu) on behalf of the foreign national.
visa_types.notes: Employer must first obtain a quota slot (or use quota-exempt Highly Qualified Specialist pathway, HQS, with minimum quarterly salary of RUB 750,000 effective March 1, 2024). Initial work visa valid up to 1 year; extendable in-country. HQS pathway allows work permits valid up to 3 years. EAEU citizens (Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan) do not need a work visa or permit. Citizens of CIS countries with visa-free regimes may also work under simplified rules.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals accepted for full-time study at an accredited Russian educational institution (university, college, or language school). Requires an official invitation from the institution.
visa_types.notes: Requires an official invitation (vyzov) issued by the educational institution through the Ministry of Internal Affairs. Issued at Russian embassies/consulates abroad. Must be renewed annually in-country via the university's foreign student office. Student visa does not automatically confer work rights; separate permission required to work part-time.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits passage through Russian territory en route to a third country. Required for most non-visa-free nationals transiting through Russian airports or overland.
visa_types.notes: Transit visa typically allows up to 72 hours (3 days) in transit. Airside transit (remaining in the international zone of an airport) may not require a visa for some nationalities, depending on passport. Overland transit always requires a transit visa or valid Russian visa. Issued at Russian consulates with confirmed onward documentation.

visa_types.name: Humanitarian/Medical Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Russia for medical treatment at a licensed Russian medical facility. Requires an invitation from the treating medical institution.
visa_types.notes: Falls under the humanitarian visa category in Russian immigration law. Requires a formal invitation from the Russian medical institution and documentation confirming planned treatment. Accompanying family members may apply for a separate private/guest visa. Extensions possible in-country if treatment is ongoing.

visa_types.name: Private/Guest Visa (Family Reunion)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to visit immediate family members (spouses, parents, children, siblings) who are Russian citizens or registered foreign residents in Russia.
visa_types.notes: The Russian host (citizen or permanent resident) submits an invitation through the local MVD office (GUVM). Alternatively, a notarised private invitation is accepted. The consulate may request proof of family relationship. Spouses of Russian citizens may subsequently apply for a temporary residence permit and follow the path to permanent residency.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of diplomatic, service, official, or special passports travelling to Russia on official government business or assigned to diplomatic missions.
visa_types.notes: Holders of diplomatic, official, service, and/or special passports of 100+ countries may enter Russia without a visa under bilateral agreements (typically 90 days per 180-day period). For countries without such agreements, diplomatic/official visas are issued gratis. Exemptions for EU, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland diplomatic passport holders have been suspended. Exact terms governed by individual bilateral treaties listed on the Russian MFA portal.

visa_types.name: Golden Visa (Investor Permanent Residence Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Grants direct permanent residence to foreign investors who meet prescribed investment thresholds in Russia, without requiring a prior temporary residence permit stage.
visa_types.notes: Established by Government Decree No. 2573 (Dec 31, 2022), effective Jan 11, 2023. Investment options include: (1) RUB 15 million in socially significant regional projects within 3 years; (2) RUB 30 million in a Russian company with RUB 6 million/year in taxes; (3) Russian commercial entity with RUB 4 million/year taxes, operating 2+ years; (4) Real estate with cadastral value RUB 50 million (Moscow), RUB 20 million (Far East), RUB 25 million (elsewhere). Annual quota: 3,802 permits for 2026. Path to citizenship after 5 years of permanent residence. Requirement to stay 6 months/year was removed in late 2025/early 2026.

visa_types.name: Shared Values Temporary Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residence to foreign nationals from designated countries who demonstrate alignment with traditional Russian values, without requiring a standard quota slot.
visa_types.notes: Established by Presidential Decree No. 702 (Aug 19, 2024). Available to citizens of 47 countries (including US, UK, Canada, most EU states; list approved Sep 17, 2024). No Russian language test or history exam required. Quota-exempt. Leads to standard permanent residency and citizenship pathway after meeting residency requirements.

visa_types.name: Highly Qualified Specialist (HQS) Work Permit + Visa
visa_types.purpose: Fast-track work authorisation for highly skilled foreign specialists sponsored by a Russian employer, offering expedited processing and longer validity than the standard work permit.
visa_types.notes: Minimum quarterly salary of RUB 750,000 required (effective March 1, 2024). Employer files with Ministry of Internal Affairs. Work permit valid up to 3 years (matching contract duration); extendable without leaving Russia. Exempt from standard labour quotas. Family members receive derivative visas. HQS status also facilitates accelerated temporary and permanent residence.

RESTRICTION: does not apply to holders of Jordanian temporary (T-type) passports lacking a national number — those holders must obtain a traditional consular visa (e-visa not accepted). Confirmed by Russian Embassy Jordan official Facebook statement Dec 13, 2025.

Bilateral visa-free agreement entered into force May 11, 2026; up to 90 days per calendar year (continuous or cumulative); covers tourism, business, family visits, cultural/scientific/economic/sports events, and transit; excludes work, study, permanent residence, and Hajj pilgrimage. Applies to all passport types including ordinary. Russia's Foreign Ministry confirmed implementation date on April 6, 2026 after domestic ratification by both governments. Saudi Arabia is the first country to extend mutual visa-free including ordinary passports to Russia.

Separate group-tour visa-free (5–50 persons, 15 days) remains per bilateral treaty. visa_policy.notes: Russia's e-visa (unified electronic visa) is available to citizens of approximately 64–66 countries as listed by the Russian Government on the official kdmid.ru portal (fetched June 2026 showed 64 countries; prior record count of 68 may have been inflated). Validity is 120 days from issue date (extended from 60 days effective August 23, 2025); permitted stay up to 30 days; single-entry; processed in 4 calendar days; fee USD 52 (children under 6 exempt). Visa-free countries are based on bilateral agreements. RuID digital pre-registration: from June 30, 2025, Russia launched an experimental electronic notification system for visa-free travelers; registration via Gosuslugi portal or RuID app is VOLUNTARY during the experiment (until Dec 31, 2027) per official Ministry of Digital Development clarifications - NOT mandatory. EAEU members (Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan) have free movement of labor. Starting Jan 1, 2025, EAEU citizens face a 90-day per calendar year cap without additional documents. China individual visa-free (30 days) is active Dec 1, 2025 – Sep 14, 2026 under Presidential Executive Order. Jordan full ordinary-passport visa-free (30 days) entered force Dec 13, 2025. Myanmar formal bilateral visa-free agreement entered force January 27, 2026 (30 days/visit, 90 days/year). Saudi Arabia bilateral visa-free entered force May 11, 2026 (90 days/calendar year, ordinary passports).

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of any valid Belarus visa (not e-visa) plus a mutually recognized identity document may enter Russia during the validity period of that visa. Period of stay calculated from date of entry into first country from a third state. Land border crossing restricted to 6 designated routes: Yukhovichi-Dolostsy, Jezerishche-Nevel, Liozno-Kruglovka, Redki-Krasnaya Gorka, Zvenchatka-Dubovichka, Selishche-Novozybkov. E-visas excluded pending settlement of technical aspects. Agreement in force since January 11, 2025.

credential.subtype: temporary residence permit or permanent residence permit (Belarus-issued)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid Belarus temporary or permanent residence permit plus a mutually recognized identity document may enter Russia during the validity period of the permit. Period of stay calculated from date of entry into Russia from a third state. Land border crossing restricted to 6 designated routes (same as Belarus visa holders). E-visas excluded. Agreement in force since January 11, 2025; agreed under Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Belarus and the Government of the Russian Federation on mutual recognition of visas, signed June 19, 2020.

credential.subtype: APEC Business Travel Card with RUS code on reverse

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals holding an APEC Business Travel Card (physical card only - virtual ABTC requires a special multiple-entry permit) with 'RUS' code on the back side may enter and exit Russia without a visa on the basis of the card plus a valid identity document recognized by Russia. Permitted for business trips only. Maximum 90 days per any 180-day period. Priority lane access at immigration checkpoints. Russia became a full APEC participant on June 1, 2013. Governing decree: Presidential Decree No. 777 of June 5, 2012. Eligible cardholders are business persons approved by APEC economies: Australia, Brunei, Chile, China, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Japan, South Korea, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Singapore, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam and other full APEC members.

credential.subtype: Alaska indigenous tribal member status with relative in Chukotka

conditional_access.conditions: Residents of Alaska who are members of indigenous populations (tribal members) and have blood relatives, tribal members, or persons sharing linguistic/cultural heritage in Chukotka Autonomous Okrug may enter Chukotka without a visa when invited by a relative. Entry limited to four designated checkpoints: Anadyr, Provideniya, Lavrentiya, and Uelen. Agreement ratified July 17, 2015. The broader arrangement dates to 1989 (indigenous peoples of the Bering Strait region). Access is restricted to Chukotka Autonomous Okrug only.

Holders of diplomatic, official, service, and/or special passports of numerous countries (100+) may enter Russia without a visa under bilateral agreements. Stay is typically 90 days per 180-day period (or 3 months per period). Specific countries and permitted passport types (D=diplomatic, O=official, S=service, Sp=special) vary by bilateral treaty. Notable exceptions: visa-free exemption for diplomatic/official passport holders of EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland has been suspended. Israel diplomatic and service passport holders require a visa. Belarus and South Ossetia have freedom of movement. Precise per-country conditions are governed by individual bilateral agreements listed on the Russian MFA portal.

Lebanon and Russia signed a visa waiver agreement on April 22, 2026 exempting holders of diplomatic, service, and special passports from entry visa requirements. Signed at the Foreign Ministry headquarters in Beirut by Russian Ambassador Alexander Rudakov and Lebanon's Foreign Ministry secretary-general Abdel Sattar Issa. Agreement enters into force 60 days after exchange of ratification notifications; remains valid for five years and automatically renewed unless suspended. Stay limits not specified in available text. NOTE: Agreement signed but NOT YET IN FORCE as of validation date.

cbi.notes: Russia does not have a formal citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Citizenship can be pursued after 5 years of permanent residency. The investor/golden visa (Government Decree No. 2573, Dec 31, 2022) grants permanent residence directly but does not grant citizenship. No official CBI program exists.

programs.name: Russian Golden Visa (Investor Residence Permit)
programs.notes: Established by Government Decree No. 2573 (Dec 31, 2022), effective Jan 11, 2023. Grants direct permanent residence (not temporary residence first). Investment options: (1) RUB 15 million in socially significant regional projects within 3 years; (2) RUB 30 million in a Russian company operating 3+ years and paying RUB 6 million/year in taxes; (3) Registration of a Russian commercial entity with RUB 4 million/year taxes paid, operating 2+ years; (4) Real estate investment with cadastral value: RUB 50 million (Moscow), RUB 20 million (Far East), RUB 25 million (elsewhere) - new build under 2 years old, held 1+ year before applying. Total annual quotas: 3,802 for 2026. Requirement to stay 6 months/year in Russia was removed in late 2025/early 2026.

programs.name: Shared Values Visa (Temporary Residence Permit)
programs.notes: Presidential Decree No. 702 (Aug 19, 2024). Grants temporary residence permit (TRP) without quota for foreigners who align with traditional Russian values. Available to citizens of 47 countries (including US, UK, Canada, most EU states - list approved Sep 17, 2024 by PM Mishustin). No Russian language test or history exam required. Path to PR and citizenship follows standard TRP route.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Specialist (HQS) Work Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with specialized skills; minimum quarterly salary of RUB 750,000 (effective March 1, 2024); employer must file with Ministry of Internal Affairs
programs.processing_time: 14–30 business days; exempt from standard labor quotas
programs.notes: Work permit valid up to 3 years (duration of contract); unlimited extensions without leaving Russia. Employer-sponsored; employer submits application to MVD with employment contract.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Visa (New Pathway, effective April 15, 2026)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals in science, economics, industry, education, culture, business, or sports; no Russian language test required; no immigration quota
programs.notes: Presidential Decree No. 883 (December 2025). Effective April 15, 2026. Eligible candidates can obtain 3-year temporary residency or apply directly for Permanent Residency. Application submitted through authorized agency digitally from home country; approved applicants receive 1-year business visa with immediate work rights. Family members included.

agreements: EAEU (Eurasian Economic Union) free movement of labor - Russia, Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan
CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) open borders/freedom of movement provisions

outbound_official.notes: Official source: Consular Information Portal of the Russian MFA (kdmid.ru), page titled 'Information about visa/visa-free travel regime for citizens with various passport types established through international agreements and unilateral decisions', as of 2026-05-08. Lists regimes for ordinary passport (общегражданский паспорт) holders. Russia's unified e-visa (single-entry, 30-day stay, 120-day validity, USD 52 fee) covers ~68 countries per electronic-visa.kdmid.ru. visa_on_arrival is empty - Russia does not operate a general visa-on-arrival outbound programme (these are destination-side VOA schemes). Some countries appear in both visa_free and e_visa where e-visa remains available as an alternative pathway. Albania, Algeria, Benin, Bulgaria, Bahrain listed as visa-free may apply only to diplomatic/service passports for ordinary-passport holders - full ordinary-passport verification was limited by page truncation; cross-referenced from existing visa_policy field in this file.

The complete official visa-free country list with specific max-stay days per bilateral agreement could not be directly scraped from mid.ru due to connection errors - the visa-free entries are cross-referenced from mid.ru sub-domains and corroborated by multiple official Russian embassy sources but individual bilateral treaty texts were not retrieved.
Ukraine is listed in some sources as having visa-free access but given current geopolitical situation the status is unclear; omitted.
Abkhazia and South Ossetia visa-free entries omitted as these are contested territories not universally recognized.

Georgia (GEO) unlimited-stay visa-free was sourced from secondary sources (gw2ru.com) - direct official Russian MFA page for Georgia could not be fetched; treat as medium-confidence until verified from mid.ru directly.
The 'Shared Values Visa' list of 47 eligible countries was not independently verified from an official government domain.

Golden Visa (Decree No. 2573) min_amount shown is the lowest threshold (RUB 15M for socially significant projects); full investment criteria details are from secondary legal sources, not the decree text itself.
Russia does not have a digital nomad visa or ETA scheme as of available information.
The new Skilled Visa (Presidential Decree No. 883, April 2026) was verified via multiple immigration news sources referencing kremlin.ru but the kremlin.ru page itself timed out during direct access.
Visa-on-arrival: Russia does not operate a general visa-on-arrival program; omitted.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - Alaska indigenous (Chukotka): The special visa-free arrangement for Alaska indigenous residents visiting Chukotka was not verifiable from a live official Russian government domain; sourced from Wikipedia. Treat as low-confidence.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS - Diplomatic/service passport holders (general): Full official per-country list could not be fetched from mid.ru directly. Entry sourced from Wikipedia.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS - Lebanon diplomatic passport waiver (April 22, 2026): Agreement signed but not yet in force; specific stay limits not published.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS - No OCI cardholder exemption found: Russia does not appear to offer any special conditional entry benefit for India OCI cardholders based on OCI status alone.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - No US/UK/Schengen visa-based entry confirmed: Some aggregators claimed holders of valid US or Schengen multiple-entry visas can enter Russia visa-free for 15 days, but no official Russian government domain confirmed this rule. Not recorded.
Macao SAR max_stay updated to 30 days but could not be verified from live hongkong.mid.ru (connection failed); sourced from secondary sources.

North Korea (PRK) e-visa: Re-added based on confirmed issuance data. Official kdmid.ru/country.html showed PRK as of June 2026 fetch, and Korean Times confirmed 710 e-visas issued in 2025. However, practical travel to Russia for North Koreans may be further restricted by DPRK exit controls - the e-visa reflects Russia's admission policy only.

Saudi Arabia (SAU) visa-free: Confirmed from multiple press sources citing Russian MFA statement effective May 11, 2026. Direct official Russian MFA source (mid.ru) was unavailable due to connection errors; treat as high-confidence secondary confirmation.

E-visa eligible country count: Fetched page showed 64 countries (vs prior count of 68); discrepancy may be due to some countries being shown separately or removed. Exact current count unresolved without a direct scrape of the full list.

visa_types: Standard consular visa fees (business, work, student, transit, medical, family) vary by nationality and processing speed and could not be confirmed from official sources fetched; set to null.
visa_types: Transit visa 72-hour limit is standard Russian practice but the specific official page confirming this could not be fetched due to mid.ru connection issues.
digital_nomad visa: Russia has no official digital nomad visa category as of June 2026.
